seo.title: Building Data Extraction with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction pipelines with LangChain enables structured output from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls, powering applications like invoice processing and contract analysis. Guardrails are essential to ensure schema compliance, field accuracy, and completeness, preventing costly downstream errors from hallucinations or inconsistent formatting. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they impact your production systems.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
The typical LangChain data extraction architecture starts with document loaders that ingest unstructured text (PDFs, emails, web pages). Text is split into chunks and embedded using models like OpenAI embeddings, then passed to an LLM chain configured with Pydantic/output parsers for schema-guided extraction. A prompt template defines the extraction schema, and the LLM generates structured JSON output validated against the defined model.

For complex documents, retrieval chains (RAG) may fetch relevant context, while agents with tools handle multi-step extraction. Output parsers like PydanticOutputParser ensure type consistency, with fallbacks for parsing errors. This architecture scales to production but is vulnerable to hallucination, schema drift, and context overflow.

Vex wraps the chain.invoke() call to verify outputs against predefined checks for completeness, accuracy, and format compliance before forwarding to downstream systems.

risks.name: Schema Non-Compliance
risks.description: LLM generates JSON missing required fields or with incorrect structure
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation + Pydantic fallback parsing

risks.name: Field Hallucination
risks.description: LLM invents non-existent values (wrong invoice amounts, dates)
risks.mitigation: Vex field accuracy checks with regex/pattern matching

risks.name: Incomplete Extraction
risks.description: Required fields left null or empty despite being present in document
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness validation across all schema fields

risks.name: Type Inconsistency
risks.description: String dates parsed as numbers, missing type coercion
risks.mitigation: Vex type consistency checks + strict output parsing

implementation.title: Basic Schema-Guided Extraction (Python)
implementation.description: Define Pydantic model and create extraction chain with structured output
implementation.code:
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from langchain.output_parsers import PydanticOutputParser
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(description="Unique invoice ID")
    date: str = Field(description="Invoice date")
    amount: float = Field(description="Total amount")
    vendor: str = Field(description="Vendor name")

parser = PydanticOutputParser(pydantic_object=Invoice)
prompt = PromptTemplate(
    template="Extract invoice data: {format_instructions}\nDocument: {document}",
    input_variables=["document"],
    partial_variables={"format_instructions": parser.get_format_instructions()},
)
llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini", temperature=0)
chain = prompt | llm | parser

result = chain.invoke({"document": "Invoice #INV-1234 dated 2024-03-15 from Acme Corp: $2,500"})
print(result)

implementation.description: Zod schema validation with LangChain.js structured output

implementation.title: RAG-Enhanced Extraction (Python)
implementation.description: Combine retrieval with extraction for long documents
implementation.code:
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

# Assume documents loaded and split
text_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=1000, chunk_overlap=200)
splits = text_splitter.split_documents(documents)
vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(splits, OpenAIEmbeddings())
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

extraction_chain = prompt | llm | parser
rag_chain = ({"context": retriever, "document": RunnablePassthrough()}) | extraction_chain

result = rag_chain.invoke("invoice #1234")

implementation.description: Process multiple documents with parallel execution

vexIntegration.title: Wrap chain.invoke() with Vex Verify (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Single line integration catches all extraction failures
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Your existing LangChain chain
result = chain.invoke({"document": document_text})

# Vex verification
verified = vex.verify(
    result,
    checks=[
        vex.schema_matches(Invoice),
        vex.field_present("invoice_number"),
        vex.field_matches_regex("date", r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$"),
        vex.field_positive("amount"),
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)

if verified:
    # Safe to process
    save_to_database(verified)
else:
    # Handle failure
    log_extraction_error(document_id)
